text: Difficulty migrating/importing historical data from previous versions or competitors
Complex software with steep learning curves that require significant research
Lack of reliable customer support when encountering problems
Comprehensive data protection and security features
Reliable data backup and recovery capabilities

Poor customer support (slow, ineffective, difficult to reach)
Reliable, uninterrupted access to critical business software (offline capability, uptime)

Product instability and bugs causing system performance issues (e.g., software broke, created registry errors, hosed up PC)
Poor technical support: slow response, unhelpful agents, difficult to reach
Software being overly complex or 'boggy' (resource-heavy, slowing down system)
Lightweight security software that doesn't bog down system performance

Data overconsumption from default HD video settings
Outdated apps incompatible with new device versions
Reliable threat detection and malware removal
Seamless data migration and import capabilities
Uncertainty about best practices for implementing AI policies
Lack of ready-made templates or guidance for AI usage policies
Exposure of sensitive/PII data in AI interactions leading to potential leaks
Clear, enforceable AI usage policies that balance security and development progress
Integration of AI security tools with existing identity providers for access control
High data ingestion costs and unpredictable scaling expenses
Complex, time-consuming deployment and configuration requiring significant in-house expertise
Inadequate detection capabilities from previous solutions, missing threats that competitors found
Quick time-to-value and faster return on investment (ROI)
Comprehensive threat detection that finds what other solutions miss
Lack of visibility into AI tool usage and data flows
Fear of data leakage through AI prompts (PII, sensitive data, intellectual property)
Inability to enforce security policies on AI usage across the organization
Real-time monitoring and detection of AI usage in the browser
Visibility into input and output of enterprise AI tools for incident response teams
High-volume low-context alerts burn analysts out.
False positives overwhelm security teams, losing visibility in important areas.
Difficulty triaging true alerts; not knowing the severity or context (e.g., how many hops from critical data).
Achieve the right balance between alert noise and useful signal through tuning.
Detect one or two attack stages with high fidelity and respond in time.
Fear of external LLM data breaches exposing sensitive company information
Frustration with employees circumventing security policies to use unauthorized external AI tools
Concern about being forced to use inferior internal AI tools that hinder productivity and competitiveness
Secure, internally hosted LLM solution that matches or exceeds the capability of external tools (e.g., Claude)
Clear policy enforcement to block all unauthorized external AI resource usage

Unreliable software with frequent crashes, freezes, or force closes disrupting workflow

Fast time to value and reduced setup/maintenance overhead for lean teams
Robust and easy setup with different data sources for comprehensive monitoring
High cost of log ingestion (especially over 10GB/day) and unclear pricing renewal impact.
Lack of predefined rules and integrations for specific platforms (e.g., AWS VPCFlow, Fortigate).
Complexity and steep learning curve for newer or more comprehensive security tools.
Comprehensive, 'one person army' SIEM with extensive query and rule capabilities.
Cost-effective solution with transparent pricing and manageable log storage.
Difficulty in centralizing and forwarding all logs to SIEM
Concern over high hardware requirements impacting system performance
Frustration with complex installation or configuration processes
Seamless integration of log forwarding to a central SIEM without intermediate steps
Efficient system performance with minimal resource consumption
High license and operational costs, especially with scaling data volumes
Complex and time-consuming setup/engineering for non-native data sources or custom integrations
Unpredictable/hidden costs from storage, retention, or pipeline management
Reduce total cost of ownership through efficient licensing and tiered storage
Simplify log ingestion and pipeline management, especially for diverse/on-prem sources
Excessive log volume and high data ingestion costs
Difficulty justifying new infrastructure investments to management
Fear of product upgrades breaking existing functionality or introducing bugs
Reduce Splunk license costs by filtering or rerouting low-value logs before ingestion
Maintain stable, reliable system performance without disruptive upgrades
Difficulty in logging in or switching accounts after subscription changes on devices
Missed malware detection leading to security incidents
Deception through unwanted software bundling (e.g., toolbars)
Dependable, fast, glitch-free performance
Quick, easy learning curve
Malware sophistication outpacing detection, leading to system compromise.
API key exposure risk due to misunderstanding of key IDs vs. secret keys.
Frustration with products requiring always-on internet connections.
Real-time threat detection and automated mitigation (e.g., automatic key revocation).
Clear differentiation between public identifiers and secret keys to prevent security panic.
Software instability and crashes causing workflow disruptions
Difficulty managing and administering software licenses across multiple devices
Excessive ad networks, analytics, and in-app billing in paid software, raising security and privacy concerns
Software that is stable, reliable, and does not crash unexpectedly
Clear, intuitive user interfaces that minimize the learning curve
Difficulty closing ads due to tiny 'X' button (especially for users with physical limitations like arthritis)
In-app purchase failures leading to lost money and data after reinstall
Content repetition in games (e.g., questions/answers looping after level 40)
Effective AI usage monitoring and threat detection for organizational security
Clear AI governance policies with enforcement mechanisms
Unable to authenticate/login due to confusing QR/setup process
Poor/no response from customer support for trial requests
Notifications disabled or not working after updates
Mobile visibility and control over security environment for on-the-go monitoring
Reliable alert notifications for immediate threat response
Frustration with outdated security policies like mandatory password expiration that are still taught/enforced despite being known as ineffective for years.
Annoyance at users who don't care about learning or following security best practices, creating compliance and risk challenges.
Difficulty with tools that are not interoperable with professional ecosystems (e.g., Publisher files not usable by agencies/web hosts), forcing extra work or learning new software.
Seamless integration and compatibility across platforms/devices (e.g., Microsoft 365 syncing between desktop and app).
Effective, modern security practices that replace outdated ones like password expiration, focusing on MFA, protection, and detection.
Password policies are dictated by external forces like cyber insurance and certifications, not security best practices.
Implementing modern security standards (like NIST) faces internal resistance and bureaucratic inertia.
Monitoring solutions for breaches are unreliable with poor signal-to-noise ratios.
Security policies based on evidence and modern standards (like NIST), not outdated compliance.
Reliable, actionable threat detection and breach monitoring with low false positives.
Frustration with outdated password policies that enforce arbitrary length/character rules and frequent changes, ignoring actual security
Annoyance with password meters that misjudge strong passphrases as weak, eroding trust in security systems
Irritation with hidden costs or 'bait-and-switch' tactics in apps that appear free but require subscriptions or in-app purchases
Security tools that align with modern best practices (e.g., long passphrases, no arbitrary complexity rules) and accurately assess real risk
Streamlined, transparent access without hidden paywalls or mandatory external subscriptions for core functionality
Companies implementing password policy changes without foundational security maturity, leading to 'self inflicted wounds'
Clients move slowly; security professionals must adapt to their pace, not the latest guidance
Password resets enforced without MFA compliance, causing frustration and poor security
Implement password policy changes only after foundational controls (secure storage, breach detection, blocklists, rate limiting, strong requirements, education, MFA, logging) are in place
Adopt modern security tools (strong MFA, passkeys, password filters, password managers) to reduce reliance on passwords
Frustration with low user-friendliness and complex setup processes
Anxiety over security vulnerabilities from password policies and credential leaks
Disappointment with incomplete features or platform-specific limitations
Seamless, passwordless authentication with high security and low user friction
Effective AI-driven monitoring for credential leaks and adaptive threat response

Here we go again with hidden pricing that probably scales up dramatically once you're committed. After dealing with high cost and overhead of legacy SIEM platforms, I need transparent pricing that shows real value, not another vendor trying to lock us in.

These user testimonials better mention actual integration experiences, not just marketing fluff. I've been burned too many times by software compatibility issues that weren't disclosed until after implementation started.

This navigation is already giving me problems - if I can't easily find their technical documentation, how responsive is their support going to be when we have real issues? Poor technical support response is not something I can afford.

Another signup form asking for everything except my blood type. Given how many companies have API key exposure risks due to poor data handling, I'm not filling this out until I see their actual security practices documented.

Finally, a vendor that actually addresses the inability to find quality alternatives by showing specific comparisons with metrics. If they can deliver on reducing alert noise while maintaining detection accuracy, this might be worth the evaluation effort.
